messages.content: You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Azure to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on Azure VMs to OCI Compute migration.
Como migrar Azure VMs para OCI Compute Instances?

Para migrar Azure VMs para OCI Compute:

**Pré-requisitos:**
1. Criar VCN com subnets no OCI
2. Configurar IAM policies para Compute
3. Preparar imagens Azure para exportação

**Método 1: Export VHD e importar no OCI**
```
# 1. Desalocar a VM no Azure
az vm deallocate --resource-group meu-rg --name minha-vm

# 2. Generalizar a VM
az vm generalize --resource-group meu-rg --name minha-vm

# 3. Exportar VHD para storage account
az storage blob copy start \
  --destination-blob minha-vm.vhd \
  --destination-container vhds \
  --account-name mystorageacct \
  --source-uri "https://<source>.blob.core.windows.net/vhds/minha-vm-osdisk.vhd"

# 4. Download do VHD
az storage blob download \
  --container-name vhds \
  --name minha-vm.vhd \
  --file ./minha-vm.vhd

# 5. Upload para OCI Object Storage
oci os object put \
  --bucket-name migration-bucket \
  --name minha-vm.vhd \
  --file ./minha-vm.vhd

# 6. Importar como custom image no OCI
oci compute image import \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --display-name "azure-migrated-vm" \
  --source-image-type VHD \
  --launch-mode PARAVIRTUALIZED \
  --source-uri "https://objectstorage.us-ashburn-1.oraclecloud.com/n/<namespace>/b/migration-bucket/o/minha-vm.vhd"

# 7. Criar instância com a imagem importada
oci compute instance launch \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --shape VM.Standard.E4.Flex \
  --source-details '{"sourceType": "image", "imageId": "ocid1.image.oc1..exampleID"}'
```

**Mapeamento Azure → OCI:**
- Azure VM Size → OCI Shape
- Azure Managed Disk → OCI Block Volume
- Azure VNet → OCI VCN
- Azure NSG → OCI Security List / NSG

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Migration/overview.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Azure to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on Azure VMs to OCI Compute migration.
Como mapear Azure VM sizes para OCI shapes equivalentes?

Mapeamento de Azure VM sizes para OCI shapes:

**General Purpose:**
| Azure | OCI Shape | OCPU | Memory (GB) |
|-------|-----------|------|-------------|
| Standard_B1s | VM.Standard.E4.Flex | 1 | 1 |
| Standard_B2s | VM.Standard.E4.Flex | 2 | 4 |
| Standard_D2s_v3 | VM.Standard.E4.Flex | 2 | 8 |
| Standard_D4s_v3 | VM.Standard.E4.Flex | 4 | 16 |
| Standard_D8s_v3 | VM.Standard.E4.Flex | 8 | 32 |
| Standard_D16s_v3 | VM.Standard.E4.Flex | 16 | 64 |

**Compute Optimized:**
| Azure | OCI Shape | OCPU | Memory (GB) |
|-------|-----------|------|-------------|
| Standard_F2s_v2 | VM.Standard.E4.Flex | 2 | 4 |
| Standard_F4s_v2 | VM.Standard.E4.Flex | 4 | 8 |
| Standard_F8s_v2 | VM.Standard.E4.Flex | 8 | 16 |

**Memory Optimized:**
| Azure | OCI Shape | OCPU | Memory (GB) |
|-------|-----------|------|-------------|
| Standard_E2s_v3 | VM.Standard.E4.Flex | 2 | 16 |
| Standard_E4s_v3 | VM.Standard.E4.Flex | 4 | 32 |
| Standard_E8s_v3 | VM.Standard.E4.Flex | 8 | 64 |

**ARM-based (custo-benefício):**
| Azure | OCI Shape | OCPU | Memory (GB) |
|-------|-----------|------|-------------|
| Standard_D2ps_v5 | VM.Standard.A1.Flex | 2 | 4 |
| Standard_D4ps_v5 | VM.Standard.A1.Flex | 4 | 8 |

**OCI Flex Shapes:**
A grande vantagem do OCI é a flexibilidade:
```
oci compute instance launch \
  --shape VM.Standard.E4.Flex \
  --shape-config '{"ocpus": 4, "memoryInGBs": 32}'
```

**Notas:**
- Azure Burst VMs (B-series) não têm equivalente direto no OCI
- OCI Flex shapes permitem ajustar OCPU e memory independentemente
- [MUTABLE] Preços variam por região e shape

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Compute/References/computeshapes.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Azure to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on Azure VMs to OCI Compute migration.
Como migrar managed disks do Azure para OCI Block Volumes?

Para migrar Azure Managed Disks para OCI Block Volumes:

**1. Exportar managed disk do Azure:**
```
# Gerar SAS URL para download
az disk grant-access \
  --resource-group meu-rg \
  --name meu-disk \
  --access-level Read \
  --duration-in-seconds 86400

# Obter o SAS URL
SAS_URL=$(az disk show \
  --resource-group meu-rg \
  --name meu-disk \
  --query 'accessSas' -o tsv)

# Download do VHD
az storage blob copy start \
  --destination-blob meu-disk.vhd \
  --destination-container exports \
  --account-name mystorageacct \
  --source-uri "$SAS_URL"

# Revoke access
az disk revoke-access \
  --resource-group meu-rg \
  --name meu-disk
```

**2. Upload para OCI Object Storage:**
```
az storage blob download \
  --container-name exports \
  --name meu-disk.vhd \
  --file ./meu-disk.vhd

oci os object put \
  --bucket-name migration-bucket \
  --name meu-disk.vhd \
  --file ./meu-disk.vhd
```

**3. Criar Block Volume no OCI:**
```
oci bv volume create \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --availability-domain AD-1 \
  --display-name "migrated-azure-disk" \
  --size-in-gbs 128 \
  --source-details '{
    "type": "objectStorageTuple",
    "namespaceName": "<namespace>",
    "bucketName": "migration-bucket",
    "objectName": "meu-disk.vhd"
  }'
```

**4. Attach ao Compute Instance:**
```
oci compute volume-attachment attach \
  --instance-id ocid1.instance.oc1..exampleID \
  --volume-id ocid1.volume.oc1..exampleID \
  --type paravirtualized \
  --display-name "azure-data-disk"
```

**5. Montar na instância:**
```
# Dentro da instância OCI
lsblk
sudo mount /dev/sdb1 /mnt/data
```

**Considerações:**
- VHD do Azure é formato compatível com OCI
- Use paravirtualized attach para melhor performance
- OCI Block Volumes suportam até 32TB [CHECK DOCS]
- Performance: VPUs configuráveis de 10 a 120 [MUTABLE]

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/cloningablockvolume.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Azure to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on Azure VMs to OCI Compute migration.
Como resolver incompatibilidade de VHD ao migrar Azure VMs para OCI?

Incompatibilidades de VHD são comuns na migração Azure → OCI:

**Problemas comuns e soluções:**

**1. VHD dinâmico vs fixo:**
OCI prefere VHD de tamanho fixo.
```
# Converter VHD dinâmico para fixo
qemu-img convert -f vpc -O raw meu-disk.vhd meu-disk-fixed.raw
qemu-img convert -f raw -O vpc -o subformat=fixed meu-disk-fixed.raw meu-disk-fixed.vhd
```

**2. Drivers faltando:**
OCI requer drivers VirtIO para paravirtualized attach.
```
# Instalar VirtIO na VM Azure antes de exportar
# Linux:
sudo apt-get install -y cloud-init cloud-utils
echo "datasource_list: [ ConfigDrive, Ec2 ]" | sudo tee /etc/cloud/cloud.cfg.d/99-oracle.cfg

# Verificar VirtIO
lsmod | grep virtio
```

**3. Boot mode incompatível:**
```
# OCI suporta UEFI e BIOS
# Verificar boot mode da VM Azure
az vm show --resource-group meu-rg --name minha-vm --query 'storageProfile.osDisk.managedDisk.storageAccountType'

# Ao importar, especificar launch-mode correto
oci compute image import \
  --launch-mode PARAVIRTUALIZED \
  --source-image-type VHD
```

**4. Kernel incompatível:**
```
# Verificar kernel na VM source
uname -r
lsmod | grep virtio_blk
lsmod | grep virtio_net

# Se drivers faltando, instalar antes de exportar
sudo modprobe virtio_blk
sudo modprobe virtio_net
```

**5. cloud-init não configurado:**
```
# Instalar e configurar cloud-init
sudo apt-get install -y cloud-init
sudo dpkg-reconfigure cloud-init
# Selecionar: Ec2, ConfigDrive, None

# Verificar logs pós-migração
cat /var/log/cloud-init.log
cat /var/log/cloud-init-output.log
```

**Se a VM não bootar no OCI:**
1. Criar instância de recovery
2. Attach o boot volume como data volume
3. Mount e verificar logs:
```
sudo mount /dev/sdb1 /mnt/recovery
cat /mnt/recovery/var/log/boot.log
cat /mnt/recovery/etc/fstab
```

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Compute/Tasks/preparingimage.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Azure to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on Azure VMs to OCI Compute migration.
Como migrar Azure availability sets para instance pools no OCI?

Azure availability sets e OCI instance pools têm propósitos similares mas funcionam de forma diferente:

**Conceitos equivalentes:**
- Azure Availability Set → OCI Instance Pool + Fault Domains
- Azure Availability Zone → OCI Availability Domain
- Azure VMSS (Scale Set) → OCI Instance Pool + Autoscaling

**Azure Availability Set → OCI:**

No Azure, availability sets distribuem VMs em fault domains e update domains. No OCI, Fault Domains são nativos dentro de cada Availability Domain.

**Criar Instance Pool no OCI:**
```
# 1. Criar Instance Configuration
oci compute instance-configuration create \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --display-name "azure-migrated-config" \
  --instance-details '{
    "compartmentId": "ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID",
    "shape": "VM.Standard.E4.Flex",
    "shapeConfig": {"ocpus": 2, "memoryInGBs": 8},
    "sourceDetails": {"sourceType": "image", "imageId": "ocid1.image.oc1..exampleID"},
    "createVnicDetails": {"subnetId": "ocid1.subnet.oc1..exampleID"}
  }'

# 2. Criar Instance Pool distribuído em Fault Domains
oci compute-management instance-pool create \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --display-name "migrated-pool" \
  --instance-configuration-id ocid1.instanceconfiguration.oc1..exampleID \
  --size 3 \
  --placement-configurations '[
    {
      "availabilityDomain": "AD-1",
      "primarySubnetId": "ocid1.subnet.oc1..exampleID",
      "faultDomains": ["FAULT-DOMAIN-1", "FAULT-DOMAIN-2", "FAULT-DOMAIN-3"]
    }
  ]'
```

**Autoscaling (equivalente a VMSS):**
```
oci autoscaling auto-scaling-configuration create \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --display-name "pool-autoscale" \
  --instance-pool-id ocid1.instancepool.oc1..exampleID \
  --policies '[{
    "displayName": "scale-up",
    "policyType": "threshold",
    "rules": [{
      "metric": {"metricType": "CPU_UTILIZATION", "threshold": 80},
      "action": {"type": "CHANGE_INSTANCE_COUNT", "value": 1}
    }]
  }]'
```

**Diferenças importantes:**
- OCI Fault Domains são dentro de um AD (não cross-region)
- OCI subnets são regionais (span todos os ADs)
- OCI Instance Pools são mais flexíveis que availability sets

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Compute/Tasks/managinginstancepools.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Azure to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on Azure VMs to OCI Compute migration.
Como planejar o cutover de Azure VMs para OCI Compute?

Planejar cutover Azure → OCI requer coordenação cuidadosa:

**Fase 1: Assessment (1-2 semanas antes)**
1. Inventory de todas as Azure VMs:
```
az vm list --resource-group meu-rg --output table
az disk list --resource-group meu-rg --output table
```
2. Mapear VM sizes para OCI shapes
3. Documentar dependências entre VMs
4. Identificar VMs críticas vs não-críticas

**Fase 2: Preparação do ambiente OCI**
1. Criar VCN com topologia equivalente à Azure VNet
2. Criar subnets, route tables, security lists
3. Provisionar IAM policies e dynamic groups
4. Testar connectivity (VPN ou FastConnect)

**Fase 3: Migração de dados**
1. Exportar e migrar disks (VHD → OCI Block Volumes)
2. Migrar dados de aplicação
3. Configurar replication contínua se possível

**Fase 4: Cutover (janela de manutenção)**
```
# Runbook de cutover:

# 1. Parar aplicações no Azure
az vm deallocate --resource-group meu-rg --name minha-vm

# 2. Sync final de dados
rsync -avz --delete /app/data/ user@oci-instance:/app/data/

# 3. Iniciar instâncias OCI
oci compute instance action \
  --instance-id ocid1.instance.oc1..exampleID \
  --action START

# 4. Atualizar DNS
# Reduzir TTL 24h antes
# Atualizar A records para IPs do OCI

# 5. Validar aplicação

# 6. Remover maintenance mode
```

**Fase 5: Validação pós-cutover**
- Monitorar logs por erros
- Verificar performance
- Validar endpoints
- Manter Azure como rollback por 48h

**Rollback plan:**
- Não deletar recursos Azure por 48h
- DNS com TTL baixo (60s)
- Database replication reversa configurada

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Migration/overview.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Azure to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on Azure VMs to OCI Compute migration.
Como migrar network security groups do Azure para OCI?

Azure NSGs e OCI Security Lists/NSGs têm conceitos similares:

**Mapeamento:**
- Azure NSG → OCI Network Security Group (NSG) - mapeamento mais direto
- Azure NSG → OCI Security Lists - se preferir controle por subnet

**Exemplo de migração:**

Azure NSG `web-nsg`:
```
Inbound:
  - Allow TCP 80 from Any
  - Allow TCP 443 from Any
  - Allow TCP 22 from 10.0.0.0/8
Outbound:
  - Allow Any to Any
```

OCI NSG equivalente:
```
# 1. Criar NSG
oci network nsg create \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --vcn-id ocid1.vcn.oc1..exampleID \
  --display-name "web-nsg-migrated"

# 2. Adicionar regras de ingress
oci network nsg rules add \
  --nsg-id ocid1.networksecuritygroup.oc1..exampleID \
  --security-rules '[
    {
      "direction": "INGRESS",
      "protocol": "6",
      "source": "0.0.0.0/0",
      "tcpOptions": {"destinationPortRange": {"min": 80, "max": 80}},
      "description": "HTTP"
    },
    {
      "direction": "INGRESS",
      "protocol": "6",
      "source": "0.0.0.0/0",
      "tcpOptions": {"destinationPortRange": {"min": 443, "max": 443}},
      "description": "HTTPS"
    },
    {
      "direction": "INGRESS",
      "protocol": "6",
      "source": "10.0.0.0/8",
      "tcpOptions": {"destinationPortRange": {"min": 22, "max": 22}},
      "description": "SSH"
    }
  ]'

# 3. Attach NSG ao VNIC da instância
oci compute vnic-attachment update \
  --vnic-attachment-id ocid1.vnicattachment.oc1..exampleID \
  --nsg-ids '["ocid1.networksecuritygroup.oc1..exampleID"]'
```

**Diferenças importantes:**
- Azure NSG: regras stateful por padrão
- OCI NSG: também stateful
- Azure: prioridade numérica nas regras
- OCI: ordem de avaliação mais flexível
- OCI: pode combinar Security Lists + NSGs

**Verificar regras:**
```
oci network nsg rules list --nsg-id ocid1.networksecuritygroup.oc1..exampleID
```

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Network/Concepts/networksecuritygroups.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Azure to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on Azure VMs to OCI Compute migration.
Como troubleshootar uma VM OCI que não boota após migração do Azure?

Para troubleshootar VM que não boota após migração Azure → OCI:

**Passo 1: Console history**
```
oci compute instance get-console-history \
  --instance-id ocid1.instance.oc1..exampleID

oci compute instance get-console-history-content \
  --instance-id ocid1.instance.oc1..exampleID \
  --console-history-id ocid1.consolehistory.oc1..exampleID
```

**Passo 2: Recovery mode**
```
# 1. Detach boot volume
oci compute boot-volume-attachment detach \
  --boot-volume-attachment-id ocid1.bootvolumeattachment.oc1..exampleID

# 2. Attach como data volume em instância de recovery
oci compute volume-attachment attach \
  --instance-id ocid1.instance.oc1..recoveryID \
  --volume-id ocid1.bootvolume.oc1..exampleID \
  --type paravirtualized

# 3. Mount e investigar
sudo mkdir /mnt/recovery
sudo mount /dev/sdb1 /mnt/recovery
cat /mnt/recovery/var/log/boot.log
cat /mnt/recovery/etc/fstab
cat /mnt/recovery/etc/cloud/cloud.cfg
```

**Problemas comuns:**

| Sintoma | Causa provável | Solução |
|---------|---------------|--------|
| Kernel panic | Drivers VirtIO faltando | Instalar na VM Azure antes de exportar |
| No bootable device | VHD corrupto ou boot mode errado | Reimportar com launch-mode correto |
| Hang no cloud-init | Configuração incorreta | Verificar /etc/cloud/cloud.cfg |
| Network não funciona | Network config | Verificar /etc/network/interfaces |
| fstab errors | Referências a devices Azure | Atualizar para UUIDs |

**Corrigir fstab:**
```
# Usar UUIDs ao invés de device names
sudo blkid
# Atualizar /etc/fstab com UUIDs
sudo sed -i 's|/dev/sda1|UUID=<uuid>|' /mnt/recovery/etc/fstab
```

**Re-attach boot volume:**
```
oci compute boot-volume-attachment attach \
  --instance-id ocid1.instance.oc1..exampleID \
  --boot-volume-id ocid1.bootvolume.oc1..exampleID
```

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Compute/Tasks/accessingconsole.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Azure to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on Azure VMs to OCI Compute migration.
Como migrar Azure VM extensions para cloud-init no OCI?

Azure VM Extensions e OCI cloud-init têm propósitos similares mas funcionam de forma diferente:

**Mapeamento de extensions:**

| Azure Extension | OCI Equivalente |
|----------------|----------------|
| CustomScriptExtension | cloud-init runcmd |
| Linux Diagnostic | OCI Monitoring Agent |
| Azure Monitor Agent | OCI Monitoring Agent |
| OMSAgent (Log Analytics) | OCI Logging Agent |
| VMAccessAgent | OCI Instance Agent |

**Exemplo: CustomScriptExtension → cloud-init**

Azure:
```json
{
  "fileUris": ["https://mystorage.blob.core.windows.net/scripts/setup.sh"],
  "commandToExecute": "bash setup.sh"
}
```

OCI cloud-init:
```yaml
#cloud-config
write_files:
  - path: /tmp/setup.sh
    permissions: '0755'
    content: |
      #!/bin/bash
      apt-get update
      apt-get install -y nginx
      systemctl enable nginx
      systemctl start nginx

runcmd:
  - /tmp/setup.sh
```

**Aplicar cloud-init ao lançar instância:**
```
oci compute instance launch \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --shape VM.Standard.E4.Flex \
  --metadata '{"user_data": "'$(base64 -w0 cloud-init.yaml)'"}'
```

**Instalar OCI Monitoring Agent:**
```
# Via cloud-init
runcmd:
  - curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle-quickstart/oci-prerequisites/master/install-monitoring-agent.sh | bash
  - systemctl enable oracle-monitoring-agent
  - systemctl start oracle-monitoring-agent
```

**Instalar OCI Logging Agent:**
```
runcmd:
  - curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle-quickstart/oci-prerequisites/master/install-logging-agent.sh | bash
  - systemctl enable oracle-logging-agent
  - systemctl start oracle-logging-agent
```

**Verificar cloud-init:**
```
sudo cloud-init status --wait
cat /var/log/cloud-init.log
cat /var/log/cloud-init-output.log
```

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Compute/Tasks/userdata.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Azure to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on Azure VMs to OCI Compute migration.
Quais são as principais diferenças entre Azure VMs e OCI Compute?

Principais diferenças entre Azure VMs e OCI Compute:

**1. Modelo de shapes:**
- Azure: VM sizes fixos (Standard_D2s_v3, etc.)
- OCI: Flex shapes com OCPU e memory independentes
- OCI: ARM (Ampere A1) com excelente custo-benefício

**2. Networking:**
- Azure: VNet com subnets por region
- OCI: VCN com subnets regionais (span todos os ADs)
- Azure: NSGs por subnet ou NIC
- OCI: Security Lists por subnet + NSGs por VNIC

**3. Availability:**
- Azure: Availability Sets + Availability Zones
- OCI: Availability Domains (ADs) + Fault Domains (FDs)
- OCI: FDs são racks físicos dentro de um AD

**4. Storage:**
- Azure: Managed Disks (Premium, Standard, Ultra)
- OCI: Block Volumes com VPUs configuráveis
- OCI: Paravirtualized attach para melhor performance

**5. Images:**
- Azure: Managed Images, Shared Image Gallery
- OCI: Custom Images via Object Storage, Platform Images

**6. IAM:**
- Azure: RBAC com roles
- OCI: IAM com policies em linguagem natural
- OCI: Dynamic Groups para recursos

**7. Auto-scaling:**
- Azure: VMSS (Virtual Machine Scale Sets)
- OCI: Instance Pools + Autoscaling via Monitoring

**8. Pricing:**
- Azure: Por hora, com reserved instances
- OCI: Por segundo, com free tier (Ampere A1) [MUTABLE]
- OCI: Desconto significativo para shapes ARM

**9. Management:**
- Azure: Azure Portal, Azure CLI, ARM templates
- OCI: OCI Console, OCI CLI, Terraform, Resource Manager

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Compute/Concepts/computeoverview.htm
